name: Tofu Stir Fry
recipeIngredient: 1 tsp veggie "chicken type" seasoning (I used a bulk brand)
3/4 tsp ground ginger (I would recommend 1/2 tsp)
6 oz. tofu, cubed (I used MoriNu Lite extra firm)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, whisk together the water, dry sherry, soy sauce, cornstarch, sugar, seasoning, and ginger until well combined.
2. Heat a wok or skillet over medium heat. If using a non-stick pan, lightly coat with cooking spray; otherwise, add about 2 tablespoons of water to prevent sticking.
3. Add the thinly sliced carrots and minced garlic to the hot pan. Stir-fry for 2 minutes.
4. Add the broccoli florets and continue stir-frying until the vegetables are crisp-tender, about 3-4 more minutes.
5. Push the vegetables to one side of the pan. Whisk together the sauce mixture in a separate bowl.
6. Pour the sauce over the center of the pan and stir until thickened and bubbly.
7. Add the cubed tofu to the pan and stir everything together, coating the tofu with the sauce.
8. Cook for an additional minute, then serve hot over cooked brown rice. Serves at least 2 (I say 3-4).
